title: agonandra racemosa
intro: Agonandra racemosa, also known as the 'Guiana Cup', is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The plant's extracts have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or decoction, and its extracts can also be found in various herbal supplements and preparations.
